name: First Northwest Bancorp
summary: First Northwest Bancorp is a Washington-based bank holding company operating through its wholly owned subsidiary First Fed Bank, a community commercial bank founded in 1923. First Fed offers residential mortgage loans, commercial real estate, multi-family, commercial business, auto, consumer, and construction loans, as well as transaction accounts, savings, money market accounts, and CDs. It operates 17 locations across six Washington counties and holds limited partnership investments in fintech-focused funds.

products.name: Residential Mortgage Loans
products.description: One-to-four family residential mortgage loans originated primarily for secondary market sale or portfolio retention.

products.name: Commercial Real Estate Loans
products.description: Loans secured by commercial real estate to diversify portfolio and increase interest income.

products.name: Multi-Family Real Estate Loans
products.description: Loans on multi-family properties to diversify lending portfolio.

products.name: Commercial Business Loans
products.description: Loans to businesses to diversify portfolio and increase interest income.

products.name: Auto and Consumer Loans
products.description: Auto loans through purchased programs and purchased manufactured home loans.

products.name: Home Equity Loans
products.description: Home equity loans originated primarily in Western Washington.

products.name: Construction Loans
products.description: Residential and commercial construction loans originated primarily in Western Washington.

products.name: Transaction Accounts
products.description: Checking and transaction deposit accounts for individuals, businesses, and nonprofits.

products.name: Savings and Money Market Accounts
products.description: Savings and money market deposit products for individuals, businesses, and nonprofits.

products.name: Certificates of Deposit
products.description: Term certificates offered to individuals, businesses, and nonprofit organizations.

partnerships.description: Limited partnership investment; private commercial lender focused on lower-middle market Pacific Northwest businesses; redemption agreement signed post year-end.
